words.exampleNL: Wij wonen in een groot huis.
words.exampleEN: We live in a big house.
words.audioHint: The 'ui' is a unique Dutch diphthong - say 'ow' but with rounded lips. One of the hardest Dutch sounds for English speakers.

words.exampleNL: Er zijn te weinig woningen in Nederland.
words.exampleEN: There are too few homes in the Netherlands.
words.audioHint: The 'w' sounds like 'v'. More formal than 'huis'. Used in housing ads.

words.exampleNL: Mijn appartement heeft twee slaapkamers.
words.exampleEN: My apartment has two bedrooms.
words.audioHint: Stress on the last syllable. Similar to English 'apartment'.

words.exampleNL: Zij woont in een flat op de derde verdieping.
words.exampleEN: She lives in an apartment on the third floor.
words.audioHint: Borrowed from English. Refers specifically to apartments in a larger building block.

words.exampleNL: Het huis heeft vijf kamers.
words.exampleEN: The house has five rooms.
words.audioHint: The 'a' sounds like 'ah'. Basic and essential word.

words.exampleNL: Onze slaapkamer is heel licht.
words.exampleEN: Our bedroom is very bright.
words.audioHint: Compound of 'slaap' (sleep) + 'kamer' (room). The 'aa' is a long 'ah'.

words.exampleNL: We eten altijd in de keuken.
words.exampleEN: We always eat in the kitchen.
words.audioHint: The 'eu' is a unique Dutch sound - round lips for 'oo' but say 'uh'. The final 'en' is reduced.

words.exampleNL: De badkamer is net gerenoveerd.
words.exampleEN: The bathroom has just been renovated.
words.audioHint: Compound of 'bad' (bath) + 'kamer' (room). The 'a' sounds like 'ah'.

words.exampleNL: De woonkamer is heel gezellig.
words.exampleEN: The living room is very cozy.
words.audioHint: Compound of 'woon' (living) + 'kamer' (room). The 'oo' sounds like 'oh'.

words.exampleNL: Waar is het toilet?
words.exampleEN: Where is the toilet?
words.audioHint: French-influenced pronunciation. Stress on the second syllable.

words.exampleNL: We hebben een mooie tuin achter het huis.
words.exampleEN: We have a beautiful garden behind the house.
words.audioHint: The 'ui' sounds like 'ow' with rounded lips. Dutch people love their gardens.

words.exampleNL: De huur is achthonderd euro per maand.
words.exampleEN: The rent is eight hundred euros per month.
words.audioHint: The 'uu' sounds like 'oo' in 'moon' but with lips pushed more forward.

words.exampleNL: Wij huren een appartement in het centrum.
words.exampleEN: We rent an apartment in the city center.
words.audioHint: The 'uu' sounds like 'oo' in 'moon'. Regular verb.

words.exampleNL: De verhuurder repareert het dak.
words.exampleEN: The landlord repairs the roof.
words.audioHint: The 'v' sounds like 'f'. Derived from 'verhuren' (to rent out).

words.exampleNL: De huurder betaalt de huur op de eerste van de maand.
words.exampleEN: The tenant pays rent on the first of the month.
words.audioHint: Derived from 'huren' (to rent). The 'uu' sounds like 'oo' in 'moon'.

words.exampleNL: Wij willen een huis kopen.
words.exampleEN: We want to buy a house.
words.audioHint: The 'o' sounds like 'oh'. Very relevant in the Dutch housing market.

words.exampleNL: Wij hebben een hypotheek bij de bank.
words.exampleEN: We have a mortgage at the bank.
words.audioHint: The 'th' is not like English 'th' - it's just a 't'. Stress on the last syllable.

words.exampleNL: Ik woon op de tweede verdieping.
words.exampleEN: I live on the second floor.
words.audioHint: The 'v' sounds like 'f'. The 'ie' sounds like 'ee'. Note: ground floor is 'begane grond', first floor is 'eerste verdieping'.

words.exampleNL: De slaapkamer is boven de trap.
words.exampleEN: The bedroom is up the stairs.
words.audioHint: Short 'a' sound. Dutch houses famously have very steep stairs.

words.exampleNL: Doe de deur dicht, alsjeblieft.
words.exampleEN: Close the door, please.
words.audioHint: The 'eu' is a unique Dutch sound - round lips for 'oo' but say 'uh'.

words.exampleNL: Doe het raam open, het is warm.
words.exampleEN: Open the window, it's warm.
words.audioHint: The 'aa' is a long 'ah'. Dutch homes famously have large windows.

words.exampleNL: We schilderen de muur wit.
words.exampleEN: We're painting the wall white.
words.audioHint: The 'uu' sounds like 'oo' in 'moon'. The 'r' is slightly rolled.

words.exampleNL: Het dak lekt bij regen.
words.exampleEN: The roof leaks when it rains.
words.audioHint: The 'a' sounds like 'ah'. Simple pronunciation.

words.exampleNL: De vloer is van hout.
words.exampleEN: The floor is made of wood.
words.audioHint: The 'v' sounds like 'f'. The 'oe' sounds like 'oo'.

words.exampleNL: Het plafond is heel hoog.
words.exampleEN: The ceiling is very high.
words.audioHint: French origin. The final 'd' is silent. Stress on the second syllable.

words.exampleNL: We zitten op de bank televisie te kijken.
words.exampleEN: We sit on the couch watching television.
words.audioHint: Also means 'bank' (financial). Context determines meaning. The 'a' sounds like 'ah'.

words.exampleNL: Het eten staat op de tafel.
words.exampleEN: The food is on the table.
words.audioHint: The 'a' sounds like 'ah'. The 'f' and 'l' are clearly pronounced.

words.exampleNL: Ga zitten op de stoel.
words.exampleEN: Sit down on the chair.
words.audioHint: The 'oe' sounds like 'oo' in 'pool'. Not like English 'stool' - the vowel is different.

words.exampleNL: Ik ga naar bed, welterusten!
words.exampleEN: I'm going to bed, good night!
words.audioHint: The 'e' sounds like 'e' in 'bed'. The final 'd' sounds like 't'.

words.exampleNL: De borden staan in de kast.
words.exampleEN: The plates are in the cupboard.
words.audioHint: The 'a' sounds like 'ah'. Used for all types of cabinets and closets.

words.exampleNL: Zet de melk in de koelkast.
words.exampleEN: Put the milk in the fridge.
words.audioHint: Compound of 'koel' (cool) + 'kast' (cupboard). The 'oe' sounds like 'oo'.

words.exampleNL: De wasmachine is kapot.
words.exampleEN: The washing machine is broken.
words.audioHint: Compound of 'was' (laundry) + 'machine'. The 'w' sounds like 'v'.

words.exampleNL: Het fornuis werkt op gas.
words.exampleEN: The stove runs on gas.
words.audioHint: The 'ui' sounds like 'ow' with rounded lips. Many Dutch homes still cook on gas.

words.exampleNL: Doe de lamp aan, het is donker.
words.exampleEN: Turn on the light, it's dark.
words.audioHint: Similar to English. The 'a' sounds like 'ah'.

words.exampleNL: Doe de gordijnen dicht.
words.exampleEN: Close the curtains.
words.audioHint: The 'g' is guttural. The 'ij' sounds like 'eye'. Many Dutch homes famously have open curtains.

words.exampleNL: We zitten lekker op het balkon.
words.exampleEN: We're sitting nicely on the balcony.
words.audioHint: Stress on the second syllable. Similar to English 'balcony'.

words.exampleNL: Op de zolder staat veel rommel.
words.exampleEN: There's a lot of junk in the attic.
words.audioHint: The 'o' sounds like 'o' in 'gold'. Dutch houses often have useful attic spaces.

words.exampleNL: De wijn ligt in de kelder.
words.exampleEN: The wine is in the cellar.
words.audioHint: The 'e' sounds like 'e' in 'bed'. Not all Dutch houses have basements due to the water table.

words.exampleNL: De auto staat in de garage.
words.exampleEN: The car is in the garage.
words.audioHint: French pronunciation. The 'g' at the start is guttural, but the 'g' in '-rage' is like 'zh'.

words.exampleNL: Wij gaan volgende maand verhuizen.
words.exampleEN: We're moving next month.
words.audioHint: The 'v' sounds like 'f'. The 'ui' sounds like 'ow' with rounded lips.

words.exampleNL: Ik ben mijn sleutels vergeten.
words.exampleEN: I forgot my keys.
words.audioHint: The 'eu' is a unique Dutch sound - round lips for 'oo' but say 'uh'.

words.exampleNL: Er gaat iemand aan de bel.
words.exampleEN: Someone is ringing the doorbell.
words.audioHint: The 'e' sounds like 'e' in 'bell'. Same as English.

words.exampleNL: We moeten nieuwe meubels kopen.
words.exampleEN: We need to buy new furniture.
words.audioHint: The 'eu' sound is unique Dutch. Usually used in plural form.

words.exampleNL: Het tapijt in de woonkamer is nieuw.
words.exampleEN: The carpet in the living room is new.
words.audioHint: The 'ij' sounds like 'eye'. Stress on the second syllable.

words.exampleNL: Er hangt een spiegel in de badkamer.
words.exampleEN: There's a mirror in the bathroom.
words.audioHint: The 'ie' sounds like 'ee'. The 'g' is guttural.

words.exampleNL: Ik neem elke ochtend een douche.
words.exampleEN: I take a shower every morning.
words.audioHint: French origin. The 'ou' sounds like 'oo'. The 'che' sounds like 'shuh'.

words.exampleNL: Zet de verwarming hoger, het is koud.
words.exampleEN: Turn up the heating, it's cold.
words.audioHint: The 'v' sounds like 'f'. The 'w' sounds like 'v'. Very relevant in Dutch winters.

words.exampleNL: Lees het huurcontract goed door.
words.exampleEN: Read the rental contract carefully.
words.audioHint: Compound of 'huur' (rent) + 'contract'. Essential document for renting in NL.

words.exampleNL: De borg is twee maanden huur.
words.exampleEN: The deposit is two months' rent.
words.audioHint: The 'g' at the end is guttural. Also called 'waarborgsom'.

words.exampleNL: De woningcorporatie beheert sociale huurwoningen.
words.exampleEN: The housing corporation manages social rental homes.
words.audioHint: Important in Dutch housing. These organizations manage affordable housing. Long compound word.

words.exampleNL: We eten vanavond in de eetkamer.
words.exampleEN: We're eating in the dining room tonight.
words.audioHint: Compound of 'eet' (eat) + 'kamer' (room). The 'ee' sounds like 'ay'.

words.exampleNL: De fietsen staan in de schuur.
words.exampleEN: The bikes are in the shed.
words.audioHint: The 'sch' sounds like 'skh'. The 'uu' sounds like 'oo' in 'moon'.

words.exampleNL: De makelaar laat ons het huis zien.
words.exampleEN: The real estate agent shows us the house.
words.audioHint: The 'aa' is a long 'ah'. Important for finding housing in the Netherlands.

words.exampleNL: De wastafel in de badkamer is nieuw.
words.exampleEN: The bathroom sink is new.
words.audioHint: Compound of 'was' (wash) + 'tafel' (table). The 'w' sounds like 'v'.

words.exampleNL: De huurprijzen in Amsterdam zijn heel hoog.
words.exampleEN: The rental prices in Amsterdam are very high.
words.audioHint: Compound of 'huur' (rent) + 'prijs' (price). The 'ij' sounds like 'eye'.
